g.data-narration: Welcome to ARM Firmware Suite - a comprehensive study of firmware development for ARM Cortex-M microcontrollers.
The ARM Firmware Suite is a comprehensive collection of software tools, libraries, and frameworks for developing firmware on ARM processors.
g.text: Complete software ecosystem for ARM processor-based embedded systems
Includes: Bootloaders, Device Drivers, BSP, Flash Utilities, Debugging Tools

g.data-narration: Key components include bootloaders for initial hardware setup, device drivers for peripherals, and CMSIS for standardized software interfaces.
ARM Cortex-M processors feature Thumb instruction set for high code density, NVIC for interrupt management, and MPU for memory protection.
The bootloader initializes hardware, configures memory systems, sets up clock and peripherals, then launches the main application.
g.text: First code to execute on power-on, initializing critical hardware components

g.data-narration: ARM microcontrollers organize memory into Flash for program code, SRAM for variables, and Option Bytes for configuration.
Linker scripts define memory sections: .text for code, .data for initialized variables, .bss for uninitialized variables.
CMSIS provides standardized software layer for Cortex-M with core definitions, startup files, DSP library, and RTOS API.
g.text: CMSIS - Cortex Microcontroller Software Interface Standard
Standardized software layer across ARM Cortex-M vendors

g.data-narration: Firmware development uses toolchains like ARM GCC, debuggers like OpenOCD, and flash programmers like ST-Link.
Cortex-M uses vector-based interrupt handling with automatic register stacking, priority levels, and NVIC management.
ARM microcontrollers support Run, Sleep, Stop, and Standby modes to optimize power consumption based on application needs.
The ARM startup sequence follows: Power-on → Stack init → SystemInit() → Data/BSS init → main execution.
PLL frequency calculation: Output = Input × (PLLMUL) / (PLLDIV). Timer frequency: Timer_Freq = SysClock / (PSC+1) / (ARR+1).
This example shows Timer2 initialization with 1ms interrupts and LED toggling using CMSIS register definitions.
Avoid common mistakes: forgetting to clear interrupt flags, not configuring Flash wait states, and confusing MSP with PSP.
In summary, ARM Firmware Suite provides comprehensive tools for embedded development including bootloaders, CMSIS, memory management, and debugging.
g.text: ARM Cortex-M: Load-store architecture, Thumb instructions, NVIC interrupts
Startup: Power-on → Stack init → SystemInit() → .data/.bss → main()

g.data-narration: For exams, remember the startup sequence, memory map, CMSIS components, and linker script sections as frequently tested topics.
